title: NGE and EoE Different Endings or Different Canons
display_title: NGE and EoE Different Endings or Different Canons
wikitext:
[]: NGE & EoE: Different Endings or Different Canons?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 1:15 pm I want to be clear before going on to explain this idea: This is merely a thought experiment of my own, and I wanted to see what others thought about it and whether or not an interpretation like this had any possible grounding in the original series itself. I personally don’t hold to this as a valid interpretation of the show, but the thought occurred to me that it might still be a very plausible interpretation of the show. In other words, this idea came to me, but I don’t know what to think of it yet. Hopefully, no matter what we think of EoE’s relationship to NGE or EoTV, we can all approach this as a thought experiment and see where it leads us. The idea is this: The End of Evangelion is not an alternative ending to NGE, replacing EoTV, but rather it is an ending to an entirely different Evangelion story, one that the viewer is never told in its totality. Most of this idea comes from considering Evangeion: DEATH (True)2 , including it and everything it suggests into the canon of The End of Evangelion. We know that it is at least tied direct into EoE, since the initial release of DEATH was Evangelion: DEATH & REBIRTH , with “Rebirth” containing most of the first half of EoE’s story. (The animation was changed when the footage was reintegrated with EoE, but the scene progression and story elements that the animation depicted remained unchanged.) So let’s take a look at “Death” and see what parts of NGE it changes. After the opening of DEATH, where the film establishes itself as a clip show, we’re shown a scene where Shinji tunes his cello in a school auditorium. The title card explicitly tells the viewer that these scenes in the auditorium were to have occurred 18 months before Shinji kills Kaworu. Many have disavowed these scenes’ from the canon of the rest of the TV series, as Shinji clearly has never met Kaworu prior to their meeting in Episode 24. And, while that seems to be the logical conclusion, many of the viewers still hold that the rest of DEATH and the story elements in both REBIRTH and EoE are still canon to the rest of NGE, despite the canon discrepancies of the auditorium scene within the film specifically. From a narrative standpoint, that conclusion seems inaccurate. There’s nothing in the films to suggest that we should be looking at the auditorium scene as simply a metaphor or to be symbolic of something. In fact, there’s way to munch information about concrete times and places establishing the scenes to simply categorize them as only symbolic. That isn’t to disregard the symbolism within the scenes themselves as they go on to introduce Asuka, Rei, and Kaowru, but the title card stating that these scenes took place in a physical place within a physical time frame related to the footage of something that is undeniably canon to the story tells us that we cannot just simply write these off as events that never happened in the real world. But the discrepancy of Kaowru’s appearance isn’t the only thing that messes with the canon of the original Evangelion series. Asuka and Rei are both shown to have instruments as well; Asuka with a violin, and Rei with a viola. Whereas it can be concluded that the series simply never mentioned that Rei would have a viola in the series and that this still could be canon with the NGE TV episodes, Asuka having a violin is really weird when trying to rationalize it with the rest of the series. Asuka is a very competitive human being. This stems from a lot of things about the character that we’ve discussed to great lengths at times before. The result is that she is one of the best (if not the best) Evangelion pilot in the world. (There’s some discussion as to how much “piloting” Shinji actually does with his Unit 01, as opposed to him simply having a high sync ratio with it.) But Asuka doesn’t only boast about her skills as an Eva pilot, but about everything else she feels accomplished in. She boasts about her high grades, she boasts about her completing college, she wants to boast about having Kaji as a boyfriend just so that she can feel 100% as an adult. She boasts about being proficient about all of these non-Eva related things, but in the TV series she never boasts about her skills as a very advanced musician, something you’d think a character like Asuka would take pride in. Instead of boasting about her musical skills in a related instrument, she actually complements Shinji in very broad and generalized terms when she’s impressed with Shinji’s ability to play the cello in Episode 15. Episode 15 wrote: Asuka: Not bad! I didn’t know you played. Shinji: I’ve been studying since I was five, and this is all I can do, so I don’t have any real talent. Asuka: Perseverance is power. I’m a little impressed. Shinji: I stared studying because my teacher told me to, so I could have quit at anytime. Asuka: Then why keep going? Shinji: Because nobody told me to stop. Asuka: I should have known.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Jun 28, 2014 1:35 pm The dialogue in the Episode 24 frames belongs to who?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 1:38 pm Asuka for the first frame, Shinji for the second. (I went back and added parenthetical to make them more apparent.)

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Jun 28, 2014 1:39 pm Correct me if I'm worng but... Were Shinji talking about Kaji's death to her?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 1:41 pm In Episode 24, yes. In EoE it was a conversation about Shinji’s inability to like others because he can’t like himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sat Jun 28, 2014 4:18 pm Yeah im pretty sure the Shinji and Asuka sleeping together thing was more symbolic i honestly dont believe that Shinji and Asuka as we saw them would actually progress to a point where they could have sex, at least not at the stage of their lives they are at. Plus in the train scene some of asukas lines contradict this

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 4:50 pm The sex part is still debatable, even with the Alternate Continuity theory. I was mainly wondering if this could be used to iron out the inconsistencies DEATH has with the timeline of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Jun 28, 2014 5:53 pm I think you might have a point with DEATH being its own canon, separate from NGETV. You could argue that DEATH is not followed by End of Evangelion but by REBIRTH and that, if you're studying and discussing this alternate canon, you should be watching DEATH & REBIRTH and not End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jun 28, 2014 5:58 pm EoE and the DC retcons from Angel Rebirth are really best viewed as a separate canon. The musical quartet can't be from either for reasons already given vis à vis Asuka.

[]: Re: NGE & EoE: Different Endings or Different Canons?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:00 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: What do you guys think? Given the differences in canon DEATH suggests, can it and the following EoE be entirely different continuities from NGE?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:21 pm Compiling_Autumn wrote: You could argue that DEATH is not followed by End of Evangelion but by REBIRTH and that, if you're studying and discussing this alternate canon, you should be watching DEATH & REBIRTH and not End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:33 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: By “kai” do you mean “Director’s Cuts”? Because I’ve honestly never heard that term outside of DragonBall Z blu-rays. (A quick Google search defined “kai” as something related to harbors and ocean, so I’m still confused by the term.)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:39 pm Bagheera wrote: Er, yeah it does. The material from Death (true) 2 was ultimately incorporated into the DC edits, and is what makes the DC versions distinct from the originals.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:45 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Well, I guess that depends as well. There are some new animation in DEATH that was also used in the DC cuts, but a lot of the stuff (the angel taking the form of Rei in Episode 23’, Kaowru’s conversation with Seele in 26’) were not used in DEATH (True)2.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 6:50 pm Bagheera wrote: I think the fact that material from Death was used at all is enough to settle the matter.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jun 28, 2014 8:42 pm The DC edits were removed only after they were worked into the episodes, so the intent seems to be that they were only in Death to add these new details that would be played up on in EoE. They were basically 'update patches' needed to make the new film make any sort of sense. The idea of reading Death as first presented on it's own merits is sensible from a DotA viewpoint, though I still really don't know what to do with that string quartet. If that actually happened where and when it says it did then the canon should be WILDLY different because of how this affects the various interpersonal connections between the cast, not just slightly altered. We should see Kaworu elsewhere in the clipshow, for example.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 28, 2014 9:58 pm NemZ wrote: If that actually happened where and when it says it did then the canon should be WILDLY different because of how this affects the various interpersonal connections between the cast, not just slightly altered. We should see Kaworu elsewhere in the clipshow, for example.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jun 28, 2014 11:32 pm Continuity is the job of the guy who makes sure a glass of water has the same level in every take and a canon is what you should be shot out of if you talk about fiction in these terms, but spelt wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Jun 29, 2014 6:57 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I believe there is added dialogue between Kaworu and Shinji laid over the scene from Episode 3 where Shinji is in the training simulator with Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Jun 29, 2014 11:42 am Compiling_Autumn wrote: I think you might have a point with DEATH being its own canon, separate from NGETV. You could argue that DEATH is not followed by End of Evangelion but by REBIRTH and that, if you're studying and discussing this alternate canon, you should be watching DEATH & REBIRTH and not End of Evangelion.
